problem.statement: Awnak errands and SHEIN assisted purchases require one governed journey from client intake through missing-information exchange, quote, WLT approval, operator execution, captain assignment, delivery evidence, exception resolution and client readback.
problem.currentWorkaround: Creation and basic quote controls existed, but missing-information exchange, execution evidence, special-request exceptions and WLT-owned financial readback were not consistently available on affected surfaces.

actors.role: Owner of an Awnak errand or SHEIN assisted-purchase request
permittedActions: create owned special request with an idempotency key
answer a pending governed operator information request
approve a prepared quote through the governed WLT handoff
refuse a prepared quote through the governed cancellation mutation
read assignment delivery proof exception and WLT-owned financial status
forbiddenActions: approve a quote while the request is in customer_information

permittedActions: request missing client information with expectedVersion
prepare a quote after information review
assign an eligible request to a captain
read dispatch proof and exception evidence
record operational rejection and resolve eligible delivery exceptions
forbiddenActions: create a local-only request or exception state
use rejectionReason as a substitute for an information question

actors.role: Captain assigned to Awnak or SHEIN final-mile work
permittedActions: identify Awnak and SHEIN final-mile service type
submit proof of delivery and report a governed exception
forbiddenActions: invent local proof or exception truth

actions: read execution proof exception and WLT status
read execution proof exception and WLT status

surfaces.exclusionReason: The registry assigns JRN-022 to client, captain and control-panel; partner stores do not own Awnak or SHEIN assisted-purchase truth.
Field workforce does not create, quote, approve, execute or dispatch JRN-022 requests.

actions: link WLT dispatch proof and exception references
refresh canonical request execution and financial readback

outcome.statement: Each Awnak or SHEIN request has one versioned DSH lifecycle, governed information rounds, one WLT payment truth when quoted, one dispatch linkage, proof and exception readback on every affected surface.
outcome.primaryMetric: successful special-request mutation and evidence readback rate
outcome.baselineState: Creation and basic quote controls existed, but information exchange and post-dispatch evidence were incomplete or disconnected.
outcome.targetState: Client, operator and captain surfaces consume the same generated contract, backend state model, dispatch evidence and WLT-owned financial readback.
outcome.observationWindow: independent product, runtime, accessibility and financial-boundary review

criteria: Client creation is idempotent and validates required SHEIN or Awnak data through governed UI Kit fields.
Operator can request missing information with expectedVersion; the client can answer the pending exchange; the request returns to governed review with audit and outbox evidence.
Quote approval is available only at customer_approval and is unavailable at customer_information.
Client can list, refresh, approve an eligible quote, refuse a prepared quote through the governed cancellation mutation, and cancel an eligible owned request.
The rejected terminal state is operator-owned for operational refusal with recorded reason; client quote refusal does not create a second terminal or financial truth.
Operator can prepare a quote, apply an expected-version transition and assign an eligible captain.
SHEIN dispatch is rejected before ready_for_delivery with structured blocking reasons.
Captain inbox distinguishes Awnak and SHEIN final-mile assignments and the dispatch owner persists proof and exception truth.
Client and operator can read assignment, delivery, proof of delivery and latest exception evidence from the dispatch owner.
Special-request exceptions use the existing dispatch exception owner and support eligible retry or reassignment; cancel_order and return_to_store remain order-owned decisions.
WLT remains the only financial truth owner; DSH displays WLT payment-session readback and declares partner settlement not applicable for JRN-022.
Every mutation is followed by canonical DSH and affected-owner readback.

Binary media is not required by the intake discovery model; product URL, structured item data and address references are required. Delivery proof media remains owned by Dispatch/Media after assignment.
failureStates: information question stored as rejection reason
requiredChecks: targeted Go state-model and information-exchange tests
route registration and dispatch exception tests

business: DSH owns operational request and information-exchange state while WLT owns payment truth and Dispatch owns assignment delivery proof and exception truth.
Every successful write is followed by canonical readback.
customer_information and customer_approval are separate stages with separate client actions.
SHEIN cannot dispatch before purchase, inbound, sorting and readiness timestamps are complete.
Captain assignment retains the specialRequestId and requestType linkage.
Client quote refusal and general client cancellation share one governed cancellation mutation; operational rejection remains operator-owned.
Partner settlement is not applicable because JRN-022 has no partner settlement source.
negative: No client can read or mutate another client request.
No surface can calculate or persist final financial truth.
No local array, mock or fallback can represent runtime request, evidence or exception state.
No operator can bypass the workflow-stage model or use rejectionReason for missing information.
No captain can receive an unready SHEIN request.
No client quote-refusal endpoint can create a duplicate terminal state or independent WLT mutation.
No second special-request exception subsystem may exist outside Dispatch.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-022 code gaps without creating a second media, exception or financial subsystem, deploying production or activating commercial SaaS.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
DSH shared special-requests owns frontend business coordination.
Dispatch owns assignment delivery proof and exception truth.
WLT remains the sole financial truth owner.
No force push, release or production deployment is authorized.
